CITY OP BAYWABD • OPPICB OP TBB CITY ATTOBNBY 
Ms. Alice Hughes 
Documents Examiner 
State of California 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 95804 
Dear Ms. Hughes: 
March 31, 1981 
The City Clerk of the City of Hayward has forwarded 
to me your letter to her of March 26, 1981. 
In your 
letter to Ms. Weaver you noted that the statement of 
economic interest completed by Thomas J. Martino, a member 
of the Board of Adjustments of the City of Hayward, was 
being returned, apparently because of your determination 
that his position is not within those described by 
Government Code Section 87200. 
I am writing to advise you that the City of Hayward 
considers members of its Board of Adjustments to be 
planning commissioners for the purpose of compliance with 
provisions of conflict of interest laws. 
Members of the 
City of Hayward's Board of Adjustments filed as planning 
commissioners under the Moscone Act and were subject to a 
local conflict of interest code with disclosure requirements 
identical to those applied to planning commissioners before 
the 1979 amendment to Government Code Section 87200. 
The duties of the Board of Adjustments of the City of 
Hayward are set out in Section 10-1.572 of the Zoning 
Ordinance of the City of Hayward and include the following: 
1. 
To hear and decide appeals concerning the 
interpretation or administration of the 
Zoning Ordinance; 
2. 
To hear and decide referrals regarding the 
interpretation or administration of the 
Zoning Ordinance submitted by the Planning 
Director. 
22g00 s::nnTIooIII I 
&:lnllll:'\J.6ctr'\ 
UAVWAon o,AC:At .. IAtl:\ c:a.t _·'Ht.o1l1'!' 


Ms. Alice Hughes 
-2-
March 30, 1981 
3. 
To hear and decide conditional use permit 
and variance applications; 
4. 
To hear and decide appeals of administrative 
decisions with regard to administrative use 
permits and site plan reviews. 
It is our opinion that these duties assigned to the 
Board of Adjustments fit precisely the definition of the 
"second" planning commission contained in Government 
Code Section 65100. 
The City Clerk is presently retaining the disclosure 
statement of Mr. Martino and the statement of at least 
one other member of the Board of Adjustments until the 
City of Hayward is formally advised by the Commission that 
our determination that the Board of Adjustments is a 
planning commission is erroneous and that members of the 
Board are not governed by the provisions of Section 87200 
of the Political Reform Act. 
ACG:jp 
cc: 
Ms. Paloma Weaver 
Very truly yours, 
JOHN W. SCANLON 
City Attorney 
By 
Alice C. Graff 
Assistant City Attorney

sections.question: Are members of the Hayward Board of Adjustment 'planning commissioners' within the meaning of Government Code Section 87200, and are they required to file Statements of Economic Interest?
sections.conclusion: I agree with your legal analysis and conclusion. Mr. Martino and other members of the Board of Adjustment are Article 2 filers and are required to file disclosure statements with this agency.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether members of the Hayward Board of Adjustment qualify as planning commissioners under Government Code Section 87200 and must file disclosure statements with the FPPC.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC agrees that Board of Adjustment members meet the definition of planning commissioners in Government Code Section 87200 and are required to file disclosure statements as Article 2 filers.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request from City Attorney Graff (March 31, 1981) and FPPC response from Counsel Stella Connell Levy (April 17, 1981). Graff's letter cites Board of Adjustment duties from Zoning Ordinance Section 10-1.572. Minor OCR artifacts present but do not significantly impact understanding.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Are members of the Hayward Board of Adjustment 'planning commissioners' within the meaning of Government Code Section 87200, and are they required to file Statements of Economic Interest?

CONCLUSION: I agree with your legal analysis and conclusion. Mr. Martino and other members of the Board of Adjustment are Article 2 filers and are required to file disclosure statements with this agency.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to Hayward City Attorney's inquiry confirming that members of the City's Board of Adjustment are planning commissioners subject to Government Code Section 87200 disclosure requirements. The FPPC concurs with the city's legal analysis that the board's duties match the definition of a planning commission.
